verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase that is anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal dual lipid modification (myristoylation at Gly2 and palmitoylation at Cys3). Its catalytic domain and all functional domains face the cytoplasm. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no extracellular domain. While LYN associates with surface receptors (BCR, CD19, FcεRI) on the cytoplasmic face, this is inner-leaflet signaling — the protein body is never exposed to the extracellular space. No documented extracellular ecto-pool, cancer-cell surface display, or other contextual outer-leaflet biology has been reported. Verdict: no / inner_leaflet_anchored.
